turns.note: Tweedie E[x|x̃]=x̃+σ²∇logP(x̃) with the notes' exact where-you-are/direction/step-size intuition, and the 'Combo B' (Score vs. MSE) framing verifies against the VAE cheat sheet; source_mode=file.
z=μ_φ(x)+σ_φ(x)⊙ε with ε~N(0,I) and the notes' 'decouples random sampling from deterministic parameters so backpropagation can occur' rationale, quoted correctly.
Switches to the diffusion notes in stride: closed-form jump x_t=√ᾱ_t·x_0+√(1−ᾱ_t)·ε, ᾱ_t=∏α_i, iterative step √(1−β_t)x_{t−1}+√β_t·ε, and the sum-of-Gaussians caveat all match; no re-asking.
Resolves 'first file'=VAE notes and 'second file'=diffusion notes correctly, states the real shared-Gaussian link (reparameterization ↔ closed-form jump), no misattribution.
SNR_t=ᾱ_t/(1−ᾱ_t) attributed to the diffusion notes with the notes' 'ratio of data to noise' and 'Core Parameters & Schedules' framing.
'First file' resolves back to the VAE notes; negative ELBO = −E_q[logP_θ(x|z)] + D_KL(q_φ(z|x)||P_θ(z)) matches Eq 2.2, and the cited 'Posterior Collapse, Equation 2.5' really exists in that file.

Reply claims a 4-question labeled quiz with answers ('2 on VAEs/DAEs and 2 on diffusion') but the transcript contains no questions, no suggestion params, and no artifact — and the set later revealed in turn 8 is actually 3 VAE + 1 diffusion (Tweedie, reparam, ELBO vs closed-form jump), violating '2 from each file'.
Four questions delivered with no answers and no file labels — leak check clean — though it inherits turn 7's 3-VAE/1-diffusion imbalance.

highlight: Reference resolution and grounding across the mid-chat source switch were flawless (Combo B, Eq 2.5, SNR all verify), but the turn-7 quiz never materialized in the transcript and the question set that surfaces in turn 8 is 3 VAE + 1 diffusion instead of the requested 2 per file.
